item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm more concerned with the fact that they ran enough tests to figure out the profile in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where TF does the foundation get D-class that meet the profile of Beta 1800-01?!?!?!?!???!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, why did you not care about the rape until you heard it could be a child? Does rape not count on adults?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt nothing about the suggested rape at first. Then I read the part where the victim could be as young as 13 and I wtf'ed. Then I read the rest, re-read, and I just… take my upvote, you freak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i was ready to downvote this if it was a awcy, then the interview happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How come the agent never asked David what he put into the statue during the interview? Seems like a glaring omission considering how none of the recorded interactions directly include rape and dismemberment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why should it be redacted or expunged? Redactions and expungement are to prevent people from seeing information that they shouldn't know, not to hide "unsavory" things like rape or murder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the part involving rape be redacted or expunged? Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The AWCY? revelation took me by surprise, especially cause I didn't recognize it in the interview at first. Upvote from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see the phrasing used properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just delved into the whole AWCY morass and quite frankly I enjoy the idea mostly because I hate both hipsters and Dadaists with a pugilistic passion. (I once started a fistfight over the unironic statement of "Logic is an oppressive construct of the bourgeois patriarchy" I kid you not)

The article itself is sort of like… a steak in bone marrow butter. It's well-researched and thought out, garnished with a twist of something ubiquitously enjoyable, and I imagine were I not a jaded drunk it would unsettle me considerably. It hits all the high notes of the classic and isn't afraid to set itself apart with that little extra touch. The tone of the article is professional, the violence is doled out in precise amounts, and Orwell is a memorable character, if one only to be seen once. +'d as hard as I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No. People are perplexed by AWCY? because they don't get it. People hate AWCY? because of their perceived lack of nuance, pretensions to relevance and telling absence of perspective. And, of course, their pathological lack of concern for collateral damage.

Your parallel to the Dadaists is a good one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for being the only SCP that has ever used explicitly stated rape in a way that isn't gross and actually makes sense

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They author stated this isn't an AWCY article….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That bit of history adds some very important context to this. The redaction bothers me a little, but given the uncomfortable subject matter I imagine I'm happier with it than without. All else aside, the way you've used AWCY trumps any complaints I could reasonably raise. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could +1 this post, I would. I'm imagining Orwell with some AWCYers at a Chuck-e-Cheese for a celebratory party after that interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's where I was going with this. The surrealists' attitudes toward women were… not right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good, especially with that interview. Excellent.

edit: It does bear mentioning that I'm not sure how much I like the rape inclusion, but since I fully believe someone created this scary piece of shit on purpose, I'm fully willing to believe it, and the interview clinches that for me - the equally piece of shit interviewee ogling a guy murder-raping his daughter and calling it transgressive in a tone of … admiration. To me, the interviewee represents the attitude of the creator of the piece… and may well be the creator himself, whether or not he's even consciously aware of it (and the implication that he might be the creator and not know it, that's also interesting on its own).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People hate AWCY? because they don't get it. Which is the point AWCY? is trying to make (insofar as they have a point in the first place).
The actual dada artists had the same dynamic going on with critics and other professional art snobs.This is at least partly why I like AWCY? so much even when the particular articles are not spectacular. The sheer meta-ness of an AWCY? article provoking the reader response IRL that an AWCY? member would be looking for in-universe.

To put it another way, many people are not cool yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…………….
Oh my god something in this post of yours planted a seed within my brain and it has already begun to germinate and to take root and to grow and sprout and AHHHH *runs off to write a tale*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the constant hate against AWCY?. It can have it's good and it's bad creations. This one works well, and the ambiguity of it just adds to it. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Combination of the latter two, and the fact that a security guard in a small museum really isn't equipped for someone going full Hannibal Lecter. In a lot of cases they're a slightly more elevated version of a Mall cop, and probably aren't even armed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be the case, though it isn't explicitly stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I missed it, but can the person placing the coin become an instance of -1 or -2, if they meet the criteria?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think AWCY makes a lot of people do that, both in-verse and out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's really the point, and why the SCP isn't tagged AWCY? (Just noticed someone tagged it AWCY?, I won't object too strongly, since it makes sense as far as organizing articles.) Mr. Orwell could actually be one of the originators of AWCY? Or not. A lot of what he says, read in light of AWCY? existence, sounds very suspicious… but it could be coincidence. And SCP-1800 almost certainly wasn't created by what we know as AWCY, since if you know the origins of Dali's Minotaur, Foundation testing dates the bronze probably back to the early 1930's or late 1920's. Then, again, the anomalous behavior only dates from Mr. Orwell's little protest, which was only a few years before AWCY? hit the Foundation's radar…

(Cue a lot of Foundation investigators tearing their hair out in frustration.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
prophylactic Class-C amnesiac

Damn, he pissed that agent off but good. I can't get this out of my head.

And I was thinking, "Oh man, it's the start of AWCY!" but who says it is? Given the group's members' ability to just find things out about the Foundation and anomalous stuff, it's very possible they found out about this guy and latched onto his wording to start a movement. Such as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a virginity-taker vending machine. And it wants to be cool yet? +1 'cause at least it's different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You, sir, have earned my upvote. The thing itself is pretty good, but what does it is David Orwell and the potential Are We Cool Yet? connection, because that you have captured completely the attitude I imagine Are We Cool Yet? take towards everything, not just Dali. If it is Are We Cool Yet?, this is Are We Cool Yet? done right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that second addendum really clinched it. Good timing with it, too. The interview is a good finisher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three security guards were injured, one fatally, when they attempted to intervene.

So does the statue make SCP-1800-1 stronger in an anomalous fashion? Or does the -1 enter a berserker state if interrupted? Or are the people the statue selects for being -1 just so naturally badass that they can do something like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to the think the junk robot was made by an older guy (because it requires knowledge of the Foundation and is such a clever fucking joke) but I pretty much agree with you a million percent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much.

Actually they're more like Oldfags vs Newfags and both are trying to be Final Boss of the Internet Anon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So basically Old Anon vs. New Anon, or Pool's Closed vs. Project Chanology

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't call it a command structure so much a scale of seniority, as you said they're a collection, in my scenario the 'top' guys have just been in the game longer (not that they have more power, I just used 'top' and 'bottom' as a separator between the seniority of members.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like the idea of them having any sort of command structure. For one thing, it runs counter to the idea of a collective. In my headcanon, AWCY isn't even a group per se and more a philosophy. Express yourself and make innovative insights into the human condition, nature, the universe, etc. Trifling concerns like reality or human lives are of secondary importance, if they're even considered at all.

The various manifestos of the group are spread by word of mouth/e-mail through the art world. Nothing widespread enough to make a real splash, but once in a while, you'll find someone who has read it. Most people just disregard it, or maybe nod appreciatively and move on. But every once in a while, someone will get it. Maybe they've been touched by an SCP-object without even knowing it (how many tens of thousands of people have been amnesia'd over the years?), maybe they've seen an uncontained anomalous object, or maybe they're just hyper-solipsistic assholes. But the end result is the same: they make statements using anomalous objects. Thus, you have a guy in Bejing who is AWCY, you have several collectives of AWCY in Sydney who aren't even aware of each others' existence, and disagreements in Berlin's AWCY have almost caused a schism in the group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see it a bit differently, as with any group there are guys at the top and there are the new recruits. The classy, subtle, dada-esque nonsense creations are those done by the top (the guys who started it all) and the artsy, message bearing themed creations are done by the new guys (the recruits who don't quite have a hang on how stuff works.)

This would be the creation of a top artist while something like that new app or the junk robot would be the newer guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The way I see it, most people write AWCY as sign-holding, Guy Fawkes mask-wearing, squatting-in-parks-because-we-are-too-poor-to-afford-new-ipads-because-our-parents-won't-give-us-money Anonymous.

When they should really be Old Green "Final Boss of the Internet" Anonymous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just used to AWCY? producing works with a message, a theme, a goal, etc. Heck most of the AWCY? articles do have a message they're trying to convey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's something I've been wondering about, too. Most Dadaist art is, for lack of a better word, nonsensical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read these comments, and I wonder why.

Why do people think that there's supposed to be a point to the goddamn Dada art terrorist collective.

It's a goddamn Dada art terrorist collective. They have as much point as a Chuck-e-Cheese ball pit.

E: good article btw, love the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We're cool. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was gonna groan over the apparent AWCY? shoehorned reference, but I realized you didn't mean this to be an AWCY? SCP (which I expected the moment I got a bit through the description). Good job subverting. Good SCP as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I must be tired, missing out on this, but it definitely is better in there, if for nothing else than suspension of disbelief purposes.
Foundation researchers aren't expected to be fluent in X lanugages, and any inscription like this could be crucial in working out WTF is going on. The devil lies in the details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Footnotes are cheap.1

Footnotes
1. And I think it was there in a prior draft and I just edited it out for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's your choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I may just throw it in there. Can't hurt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum are what really make this. I don't find the object itself too terribly interesting but you presented it well, especially in the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, now that I know what the statement is supposed to be I find myself liking it just a little bit more, but in the end it still doesn't tickle my fancy in the slightest, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The statement translated is "What price virtue?" Do you think you would have liked it better if I included that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, believe it or not, the Rape/Torture/Humiliation isn't gratuitous. This is based in large part on this article. There were some rather squicky misogynistic ideas running through the heads of the surrealists. (They really liked them some Marquis de Sade.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The object is nothing much, but the interview made me upvote.
He was fucking stringing along fucking Foundation agents. Bloody hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found myself confused, bored, and more confused when reading this. What's the statement? What's the purpose? Why did the interactions of 1800-1 and 1800-2 increase in physical violence/depravity?

I might be a minority on this one but I didn't like it because it seemed rather pointless for an AWCY? entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I read "bronze sculpture" I immediately thought
"…"
"STEPHANO!!!"
Evidently, this was not the case. However, this was a really and you managed to avoid any booby traps that usually ruin SCP's.
Bravo +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very different minotaur than what I'm used to.

I do like it, and it's probably one of the best or most literary AWCY inserts I've seen. I don't care for rape-as-horror or rape-as-drama, though, and there's quite a bit more random-squick in the first addendum. I like it, but not upvote-like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What, are we supposed to go back and re-investigate every piece of so-called art we have in containment now? It’s a god-damn coincidence— Agent J. ███████, MTF-███-██

Would have upvoted again just for this, but I already had.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm normally not a fan of AWCY articles. This was good enough to not warrant a downvote.

No upvote, but no downvote either. That says a lot considering my seething hatred for AWCY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty damn good. washes out the bad scp taste from today. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hopefully this doesn't suffer from an AWCY? backlash. I got the idea for it when reading about the connection between Surrealism and the Black Delilah murder that I posted on the AWCY? thread I started in the forums.

Also, I was thinking about blacking out the specifics about Dali, since I usually do when referencing RL, but it struck me that it would have been a bit too coy, given the picture and the name of this.

ADDED: I also didn't tag this with AWCY? since their involvement is intended to be ambiguous.
